data.title: In 2025, five governments streamlined crypto licensing, including the US with the GENIUS Act and the EU's MiCA passporting.
data.description: In 2025, five governments have taken steps to streamline crypto licensing processes. These efforts include the implementation of the GENIUS Act in the United States and the introduction of the MiCA passporting regime in the European Union. These measures aim to facilitate the regulation and licensing of cryptocurrency activities.

data.title: Teva Pharmaceutical's stock reaches a 52-week high after S&P upgrades credit rating to BB+ and Moody's outlook to positive. Leverage down to 4.4x, expected to decrease further. Revenue growth after 5 years of declines, mainly from branded medicines and generics stabilization.
data.description: Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. announced that S&P Global Ratings raised its long-term issuer credit rating to BB+ from BB with a stable outlook, while Moody's confirmed its Ba1 rating and changed the outlook to positive. The upgrades are a result of Teva's ongoing deleveraging efforts and improving operational performance. S&P noted that adjusted leverage dropped to 4.4x as of Sept. 30, 2025, with expectations of further reduction to below 4.25x in the near future, supporting the higher rating. Both agencies praised Teva's financial discipline, strong liquidity, and return to revenue growth following a five-year decline, driven by the success of branded medications and stability in generics. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Contact Halper Sadeh LLC for Driven Brands Holdings Inc. shareholder rights discussion. Limited time. NEW YORK, Dec. 24, 2025 /PRNewswire.
data.description: Urgent notice to shareholders urging prompt contact with the firm due to potential limited timeframe for seeking enforcement of rights. Noteworthy confirmation dated December 24, 2025, from New York.

data.title: Investor rights law firm Halper Sadeh LLC urges Enovix Corp. shareholders to discuss their rights, fearing limited time to enforce.
data.description: Investors are urged to promptly reach out to the company in question as time may be running out to protect their rights. In New York on December 24, 2025, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm focusing on investor rights, is looking into possible breaches by specific officers and directors of Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ: ENVX).

data.title: Robinhood surged 220% in 2025 due to prediction markets. Q3 revenue was $1.27B, net income $386M. Equity trading volume reached $647B. Crypto trading volume at $80B. Customer base over 27M funded accounts with $300B in assets.
data.description: Robinhood Markets Inc. (NASDAQ: HOOD) has experienced a massive surge of over 220% in 2025, driven in part by the success of prediction markets which became the fastest-growing product in the company's history. In Q3, the company reported revenue of $1.27 billion, a 100% increase year-over-year, and net income of $386 million, up 105%. Full-year revenue projections for 2025 are between $4.6-$4.7 billion, a 55% increase from 2024, with EPS estimates of $1.95-$2.07, up 79%. Equity trading volume hit a record $647 billion in Q3, up 126% from the previous year, while crypto trading volumes reached $80 billion. The customer base expanded to around 27 million funded accounts holding over $300 billion in assets, with deposit growth exceeding 40% annually. Prediction markets, launched in partnership with Kalshi in March 2024, significantly fueled Robinhood's growth in 2025.

data.title: Lucid plans $50k crossover to compete with Tesla Model Y as it shifts focus to mass market, LCID stock moving.
data.description: Lucid Group, Inc. (LCID) is transitioning from catering to the ultra-luxury market to aiming at the mass market by introducing a new midsize crossover model priced below $50,000. This move is a strategy to compete directly with Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) and their Model Y. The company is making this shift at a crucial time as their new Gravity SUV is facing challenges. The upcoming midsize vehicle, internally known as "Project Midsize" and potentially named Lucid Earth, will be a make-or-break moment for the California-based automaker as it endeavors to expand beyond its current market. LCID stock is responding to these developments. For the complete story, refer to financial news platform Benzinga.com.

data.title: Precious metals outperformed Big Tech in 2025, with Gold up 70%, Silver up 147%, Platinum up 150%. Big Tech stocks like Alphabet and Nvidia lagged behind.
data.description: In 2025, a surprising trend emerged in the market where precious metals outperformed even the most celebrated AI stocks on Wall Street. Gold saw a 70% increase, the most significant annual performance since 1979. Silver surged 147%, its best year since 1978, and platinum jumped nearly 150%, a record high. In comparison, top tech stocks like Alphabet Inc. were up 66%, Broadcom Inc. 51%, and Nvidia Corp. 41%. This shift signifies a once-in-a-generation metals run, with silver and platinum showing remarkable growth as investors sought "cheaper" exposure. This shift in the market dynamics, with precious metals leading over tech giants, has been an unexpected but revealing turn of events in 2025.

data.title: Dow Jones rises with Nvidia reclaiming key level; biotech stock soars. Jobless claims drop, live coverage ongoing.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index showed uncertainty following an unexpected decrease in jobless claims. On Tuesday, Nvidia stock reached a significant threshold, indicating positive momentum. Additionally, a biotech stock experienced a substantial surge. The market is actively monitored with live coverage, showcasing the Dow's incremental rise and Nvidia's successful retake of a key level.

data.title: Report on Australia's mining fiscal landscape in 2025 includes governing bodies, laws, mineral licenses, and fees by state/territory.
data.description: The report titled "Australia Mining Fiscal Landscape: Regulations, Governance and Sustainability (2025)" recently added to ResearchAndMarkets.com, provides insights into the regulatory framework governing the mining sector in Australia. It delves into the roles and responsibilities of federal and state governments in resource exploration and development. The report details the governing bodies, laws, mineral licenses, and fees across different states and territories. Mineral resources are predominantly owned by the Australian government, as well as state or territory entities.

data.title: Dow Jones gains over 100 points, US jobless claims decline by 10,000 to 214,000. Market sentiment improves.
data.description: This morning, U.S. stocks showed positive movement as the Dow Jones index surged over 100 points. As of Wednesday's market opening, the Dow was up by 0.28% to 48,576.79, the NASDAQ rose by 0.02% to 23,567.10, and the S&P 500 increased by 0.15% to 6,920.17. Notably, consumer staples shares saw a 0.6% rise, while communication services stocks experienced a 0.2% decline. In recent jobless claims data, the U.S. reported a decrease of 10,000 from the previous week to 214,000 claims for the week ending Dec. 20, surpassing market expectations set at 223,000 claims. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ukraine construction industry grew by 21.7% in 2024, expected to slow to 4.2% in 2025 due to Russia's invasion impacts.
data.description: In Dublin, a report on the Ukraine construction market has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The report covers sectors such as commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential markets, with analysis up to 2029 (H2 2025). After experiencing a 21.7% growth in 2024, the construction industry is projected to slow down to 4.2% in 2025. This deceleration is attributed to the lingering effects of Russia's invasion impacting investment and business operations.

data.title: Hungary construction industry to contract by 2% in 2025 due to slowdown in real estate and industrial construction.
data.description: A report on the Hungary Construction Market projects a slight contraction of 2% in the industry in 2025, attributed to reduced activity in real estate and industrial construction. The analysis covers sectors such as Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets. The findings anticipate this decline to continue throughout 2029. The report has been incorporated into ResearchAndMarkets.com, offering insights into the market size, trends, and forecasts for the construction industry in Hungary.
